name: PyQuil (Quantum Programming for Rigetti QPUs)

auth.notes: Rigetti API key for cloud QPU access; local QVM needs no auth

performance.notes: Local QVM: ~100ms; Cloud QPU: seconds including queue

agent_readiness.notes: Quantum programming framework for Rigetti QPUs. from pyquil import Program, get_qc. from pyquil.gates import H, CNOT, MEASURE. p = Program(). ro = p.declare('ro', 'BIT', 2). p += H(0). p += CNOT(0, 1). p += MEASURE(0, ro[0]). p += MEASURE(1, ro[1]). qc = get_qc('2q-qvm') # local simulator. qc.run_and_measure(p, trials=1000). Quilc compiler: compiles Quil to native gates. QVM: local Quantum Virtual Machine. Rigetti Forest: cloud QPU access. p.define_noisy_gate() for noise models. WavefunctionSimulator: exact state vector. QuantumComputer API. Parametric programs: PHASE(gamma)(0). VQE, QAOA support via quilc. Works with PennyLane as backend.

gotchas: Requires running QVM and quilc processes locally for simulation
Rigetti QPU access requires account and may have waitlists
Quil assembly language is lower-level than Qiskit's circuit model
Rigetti's commercial viability has been uncertain
best_when: When PennyLane backend on Rigetti hardware is needed
Academic research using Rigetti's forest platform
avoid_when: General quantum computing education (Qiskit has better learning resources)
Production quantum applications (hardware access is limited)
summary: PyQuil is Rigetti's Python quantum computing SDK. Program() + H(0) + CNOT(0,1) + MEASURE(). get_qc('2q-qvm') for local simulation; Rigetti cloud QPU for hardware. Quil instruction language. quilc compiler. QVM local simulator. Apache-2.0 by Rigetti. Requires QVM and quilc processes locally. Smaller community than Qiskit. Best for Rigetti QPU access or Quil-specific research.
